blocks.text: Perfect. Now let me run the DESeq2 analysis in R:

input.code:
# Load required packages
library(DESeq2)
library(ggplot2)

# Read counts and metadata
counts <- read.csv('counts_de.csv', row.names=1, check.names=FALSE)
metadata <- read.csv('metadata_de.csv', row.names=1)

# Convert to integer matrix (DESeq2 requires counts)
counts <- as(as.matrix(counts), 'integer')

print(paste("Counts matrix:", nrow(counts), "genes ×", ncol(counts), "cells"))
print(paste("Metadata:"))
print(table(metadata$cell_type))

# Assemble DESeqDataSet
dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(countData = counts,
                               colData = metadata,
                               design = ~ cell_type)

print(dds)
